title: MOE Independent School Bursary (ISB)
text:
MOE Independent School Bursary (ISB) - SupportGoWhere
MOE Independent School Bursary (ISB)
Share link
Provides financial assistance to lower- and middle-income families with children who are in Independent Schools and some Specialised Independent Schools.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Up to full waiver of school and miscellaneous fee
Secondary students from lower income families may receive free textbooks and school attire, and school meal and public transport subsidies
Pre-university students from lower income families may receive bursary of $1,600 per annum
Collapse all sections
About this support

Up to full waiver of school and miscellaneous fees, depending on household income

Students with Average Gross Household Income of up to $4,000, or Average Per Capita Income of up to $1,000 will also receive:

Secondary students: Free textbooks and school attire, $21 transport credit per month for those who take public transport, and 10 meals per school week

Pre-university students: Bursary of $1,600 per annum

Who is this for?

Singaporean students studying in Independent Schools and some Specialised Independent Schools

Average Gross Household Income of up to $12,000, or Average Per Capita Income of up to $3,000.

How to apply?

Apply online through this link.

Where can I find help?

View more details

MOE hotline: 68722220

Submit an enquiry

Customer Service Centre 1 North Buona Vista Drive MOE Building, Podium Block Singapore 138675

Scheme last updated 27 Feb 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
